title: पार्वती कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री पार्वती कवचम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
गौरवर्णां त्रिनयनां चन्द्रमौलिप्रियां शिवाम्।
सर्वमङ्गलसम्पन्नां पार्वतीं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥

॥ कवचम् ॥
शिरो मे पातु गिरिजा ललाटं हिमवत्सुता।
भ्रुवौ रक्षतु गौरी मे नेत्रे रक्षतु शाम्भवी॥ १॥

कर्णौ मे पातु अपर्णा च नासिकां पार्वती स्वयम्।
ओष्ठौ रक्षतु कात्यायनी जिह्वां मे रक्षतु अम्बिका॥ २॥

कण्ठं रक्षतु शैलजा स्कन्धौ रक्षतु ईश्वरी।
भुजौ रक्षतु दुर्गा मे करौ रक्षतु मङ्गला॥ ३॥

हृदयं पातु शिवप्रिया नाभिं रक्षतु शर्वाणी।
कटिं रक्षतु भवानी च ऊरू रक्षतु चण्डिका॥ ४॥

जानुनी पातु महेश्वरी जङ्घे रक्षतु सुन्दरी।
पादौ रक्षतु सर्वाणी सर्वाङ्गं पातु पार्वती॥ ५॥

प्राच्यां रक्षतु मां गौरी दक्षिणे शैलनन्दिनी।
पश्चिमे पातु गिरिजा उत्तरे हिमवत्सुता॥ ६॥

आग्नेय्यां रक्षतु शिवा नैऋत्यां पातु ईश्वरी।
वायव्यां रक्षतु भवानी ऐशान्यां पातु शूलिनी॥ ७॥

ऊर्ध्वं रक्षतु सर्वेशी अधो रक्षतु पार्वती।
सर्वतः सर्वकालं च गिरिजा मां सदावतु॥ ८॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
पार्वतीकवचं दिव्यं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वापद्भ्यो विमुच्येत सौभाग्यं लभते ध्रुवम्॥

पुत्रपौत्रसमायुक्तो धनधान्यसमन्वितः।
पार्वतीप्रसादलाभात् सर्वकामानवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री पार्वती कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Parvati Kavacham ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Gauravarnam Trinayanam Chandramauli-priyam Shivam.
Sarva-mangala-sampannam Parvatim Pranamaamyaham.

|| Kavacham ||
Shiro Me Patu Girija Lalatam Himavatsuta.
Bhruvau Rakshatu Gauri Me Netre Rakshatu Shambhavi. || 1 ||

Karnau Me Patu Aparna Cha Nasikam Parvati Svayam.
Oshthau Rakshatu Katyayani Jihvam Me Rakshatu Ambika. || 2 ||

Kantham Rakshatu Shailaja Skandhau Rakshatu Ishwari.
Bhujau Rakshatu Durga Me Karau Rakshatu Mangala. || 3 ||

Hridayam Patu Shivapriya Nabhim Rakshatu Sharvani.
Katim Rakshatu Bhavani Cha Uru Rakshatu Chandika. || 4 ||

Januni Patu Maheshwari Janghe Rakshatu Sundari.
Padau Rakshatu Sarvani Sarvangam Patu Parvati. || 5 ||

Prachyam Rakshatu Mam Gauri Dakshine Shailanandini.
Pashchime Patu Girija Uttare Himavatsuta. || 6 ||

Agneyyam Rakshatu Shiva Nairityam Patu Ishwari.
Vayavyam Rakshatu Bhavani Aishanyam Patu Shulini. || 7 ||

Urdhvam Rakshatu Sarveshi Adho Rakshatu Parvati.
Sarvatah Sarvakalam Cha Girija Mam Sadavatu. || 8 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Parvati-kavacham Divyam Yah Pathed-bhakti-samyutah.
Sarva-apadbhyo Vimuchyeta Saubhagyam Labhate Dhruvam.

Putra-pautra-samayukto Dhana-dhanya-samanvitah.
Parvati-prasada-labhat Sarva-kaman-avaapnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Parvati Kavacham Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: पार्वती कवचम् में माता पार्वती के विभिन्न नामों — गिरिजा, हिमवत्सुता, गौरी, शाम्भवी, अपर्णा, कात्यायनी, अम्बिका, शैलजा, दुर्गा, भवानी, शर्वाणी, चण्डिका, महेश्वरी — के द्वारा शरीर के प्रत्येक अंग तथा आठों दिशाओं की रक्षा की प्रार्थना है। शिर की गिरिजा, नेत्रों की शाम्भवी, कण्ठ की शैलजा, हृदय की शिवप्रिया, पादों की सर्वाणी रक्षा करती हैं। जो भक्तिपूर्वक इसका पाठ करता है वह सर्व आपदाओं से मुक्त होकर सौभाग्य, पुत्र-पौत्र, धन-धान्य और सर्वकामना प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Traditionally invoked to establish divine protective armor of goddess parvati
Invokes saubhagya (good fortune) especially for married women
Protects every body part through Devi's various names
Invokes the blessing of progeny, wealth, and fulfillment of all desires
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of calamities and obstacles from life
description: Parvati Kavacham — पार्वती कवचम् — is a divine armor prayer for protection offered to Goddess Parvati. This sacred composition is sourced from Shiva Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to protect every body part through Devi's various names and create divine protective armor of Goddess Parvati. It is also said to grant saubhagya (good fortune) especially for married women. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Monday (Somvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri, Gauri Tritiya, and Hartalika Teej. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Parvati Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Parvati Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Divine armor prayer to Goddess Parvati for protection, saubhagya, and fulfillment of desires.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Parvati Kavacham and what protection does it offer?
seoFaqs.answer: The Parvati Kavacham (kavacha = armour) is a Sanskrit protective hymn invoking Goddess Parvati — the daughter of Himavat, consort of Shiva, mother of Ganesha and Kartikeya, and the manifest form of Adi Shakti — to shield each part of the devotee's body and being. The kavacham systematically requests Girija to protect the head, Himavatsuta the forehead, Gauri the brows, Shambhavi the eyes, and so on through every limb, while also granting marital harmony, progeny, freedom from widowhood, success in tapasya and the conquest of inner enemies. It is preserved in classical Shakta and Shaiva tradition and is recited especially by women devotees on Mondays and during Mangala Gauri vrata. (Sources: Devi Bhagavata Purana; Stotra Nidhi Parvati Kavacham; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Parvati and what is her significance?
seoFaqs.answer: Parvati is the daughter of Himavat (king of the Himalayas) and Mena, born after Sati's self-immolation as the same Adi Shakti reincarnated to be reunited with Shiva. After severe tapasya (the penance described in Kalidasa's Kumarasambhava and the Shiva Purana Parvati Khanda), she won Shiva as her husband, became the mother of Ganesha and Kartikeya, and is regarded as the supreme Devi in her benign aspect. She manifests fierce forms as Durga, Kali, Chandi and Bhadrakali, and benign forms as Gauri, Uma, Annapurna, Lalita, Kamakshi, Meenakshi and Mookambika. As Ardhanarishvara she is the inseparable feminine half of Shiva, embodying the Shakti without which Shiva is shava (corpse). (Sources: Shiva Purana Parvati Khanda; Kumarasambhava; Hindupedia Parvati.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Parvati Kavacham be recited for best effect?
seoFaqs.answer: Monday is sacred to Shiva-Parvati, with the brahma-muhurta (4-6 AM) and pradosha (twilight) being the most efficacious hours. Special days include Solah Somavar vrata (sixteen consecutive Mondays for unmarried girls and married women), Mangala Gauri vrata (Tuesdays of Shravana for newly-married women), Hartalika Teej (Bhadrapada Shukla Tritiya), Gauri Tritiya, every Pradosham, Mahashivaratri, the entire Shravana month, and Navaratri (especially the third day dedicated to Chandraghanta and the seventh to Kalaratri). Daily recitation facing east before a Shiva-Parvati or Gauri image, with offerings of red flowers, sindoor, kumkum and akshata, is the prescribed sadhana. (Sources: Drikpanchang vrata calendar; Hindupedia Mangala Gauri; Shiva Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Parvati Kavacham help unmarried women seeking marriage?
seoFaqs.answer: Parvati is the paradigmatic devoted wife who won her chosen husband through tapasya, making her the principal deity petitioned by unmarried women for vivaha-prapti (the boon of marriage). The Parvati Kavacham, recited alongside the Vivaha Prapti Stotram, the Swayamvara Parvati Mantra, the Katyayani Mantra and the Mangala Gauri Stotra, is regarded as removing planetary obstacles to marriage (Mangal dosha, Shani in seventh, Rahu-Ketu axis), attracting a virtuous husband (sila-sampanna), and granting the kind of harmonious union that Parvati herself enjoys with Shiva. Hartalika Teej (Bhadrapada Shukla Tritiya) is the chief vrata for this purpose, observed by both unmarried and married women. (Sources: Shiva Purana; Hindupedia Hartalika Teej; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Solah Somavar vrata and how does the kavacham fit in?
seoFaqs.answer: The Solah Somavar vrata is the observance of sixteen consecutive Mondays dedicated to Shiva-Parvati, especially undertaken by unmarried girls seeking a husband, by newly-married women for marital happiness, and by all devotees seeking general well-being. Each Monday the devotee fasts, bathes early, abhishekam-s a Shiva-linga or Parvati image with milk, water and panchamrita, offers bilva-patra, white flowers, dhatura and bhang, and recites Shiva-Parvati hymns including the Parvati Kavacham, the Lingashtakam, the Bilvashtakam and the Shiva Tandava Stotram. On the seventeenth Monday a Udyapana ceremony is performed feeding sixteen brahmins or married women. The vrata is described in the Shiva Purana and the Skanda Purana. (Sources: Shiva Purana; Skanda Purana; Hindupedia Solah Somavar.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the prescribed vidhi for Parvati Kavacham recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Bathe early Monday morning, sit on a red asana facing east, light a ghee lamp before a Shiva-linga with Parvati image or a standalone Gauri murti. Offer haldi-kumkum, sindoor, akshata, red and white flowers, bilva-patra, dhoop, deepa, naivedya of milk-sweets and fruits. Perform achamana, pranayama and Sankalpa stating the wish (vivaha-prapti, soubhagya, santati, peace). Recite Ganesha invocation, then the Parvati Dhyana shloka, then the Parvati Kavacham eleven, twenty-one or one hundred and eight times. Conclude with the Lalita Sahasranama or Devi Khadgamala, aarti and pradakshina. The forty-day mandala is the standard upasana cycle. (Sources: Hindupedia Devi vidhi; Drikpanchang; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Parvati Kavacham promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition holds that regular recitation grants soubhagya (marital happiness), avaidhavya (freedom from widowhood — the supreme boon for sumangali women), suputra-prapti (virtuous progeny), suswasthya (good health), the cultivation of feminine virtues such as patience, devotion, modesty and steadfastness in dharma, protection from all evil influences (drishti dosha, abhichara, pishacha), removal of marriage delays for unmarried girls, healing of feminine reproductive ailments, and ultimately mukti at the lotus feet of Adi Shakti. Daily recitation through one mandala of forty days, especially if combined with Solah Somavar vrata or Mangala Gauri vrata, is regarded as exceptionally fruitful. (Sources: Shiva Purana phalashruti; Hindupedia Parvati upasana; Stotra Nidhi.)
